message:
refactor(runtime): split monolithic Runtime into ServerState + ClientSession

Decompose the Runtime struct into two focused components:

- ServerState: long-lived server process state (store, AOF, TLS,
  replication, eviction, slow log, config overrides, etc.)
- ClientSession: per-client connection state (client ID, name,
  lib-name/lib-ver, no-evict/no-touch flags, auth, cluster state,
  transaction state, policy)

Runtime now composes both via `server: ServerState` and `session:
ClientSession` fields, with accessor methods for backward
compatibility. This separation clarifies ownership boundaries and
prepares for multi-client support where each connection gets its
own ClientSession while sharing a single ServerState.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
